_comment: The Lit — feature changelog / 'What's new' catalogue. This is the SINGLE source of truth for both (a) the on-page 'What's new' list + the 'New features & updates to the website' alert preview, and (b) the automated feature-update e-mails sent by lit/_scraper/alerts-mailer.mjs. ADD A NEW ENTRY (newest first) whenever a user-facing feature ships: give it a unique stable `id`, the `date` it went live (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC), a short `title` and one-sentence `summary`, and an optional deep-link `url`. On the next daily mailer run, every subscriber to feature updates whose window covers that date is e-mailed it (daily = the day it lands; weekly/monthly = batched with the rest of that period). Entries dated in the past (before a subscriber's window) are NOT re-sent, so seeding historical entries never triggers a retroactive blast. Keep the copy in sync with the About modal and CLAUDE.md, per repo conventions.

updates.title: Connect your ORCID and see your own publications & metrics
updates.summary: Add your ORCID iD to your account and — if you opt in — a new “My publications” view appears in your account menu. From there, one click takes you to your papers in the database (each showing its citation count) and to your author page in the Data Analytics spotlight (publications per year, where you publish, collaboration and disruption metrics). Everything stays private to you; your work is matched by your name, since the database has no per-paper ORCID.

updates.title: Share a saved list with anyone via a link
updates.summary: In My Library, each of your lists now has a 🔗 Share button: flip a list public and you get a link you can send to anyone — colleagues, students, co-authors — who can then open a clean, read-only view of the papers in it, no sign-in required. Only each paper's title, authors, year and link are shared; your private notes and tags never are. A public list shows a 🌐 marker, stays in sync automatically as you add or remove papers, and turning it private again immediately disables the old link.

updates.title: Hover tooltips explain the tags on each paper
updates.summary: Hover over the small tags on a paper — the journal, the year/volume, the accepting editor and area, the publication status (e.g. 'Articles in Advance'), the 'Added' date — and a short pop-up now explains what it is and that you can click it to filter. The Abstract, Significance and BibTeX buttons get the same. Thanks to the reader who suggested this on the Feedback page.

updates.title: Your account menu now appears on every page
updates.summary: The sign-in / account button now sits in the top-right of the About, Data Analytics and Feedback pages too, exactly like the main browser — so you can reach your library, e-mail alerts and default filters (or sign in) from anywhere on The Lit. The account dropdown was tidied to library, alerts, default filters, edit profile and sign out; your lists, tags and notes search still live inside My Library, unchanged. The Feedback page also gained Google Scholar and ORCID links.

updates.title: The browser opens on a curated default view
updates.summary: New visitors now land on a default filter — Management Science papers in the “entrepreneurship and innovation” area — instead of the whole catalogue, so there's something focused to look at right away. Clear the filters to browse everything, and if you sign in you can save your own default filters (journals or journal types), which take over from this one.

updates.title: A Feedback page — send screenshots and suggestions
updates.summary: There's now a dedicated Feedback page (in the top navigation) where you can tell me what to fix or add: describe the problem in as much or as little detail as you like and attach screenshots. No sign-in needed — it comes straight to me.

updates.title: Data Analytics: a citations-over-time chart and tidier filters
updates.summary: The Data Analytics dashboard gains a 'Citations over time' chart beneath publication volume (same peer-group overlays and legend toggles), the 'Editorial area trends' chart now starts at the first year with data so it's easier to read, and in the Journals and editor pickers the items you've selected float to the top of the list. A couple of redundant charts were removed to keep the page focused.

updates.title: E-mail alerts moved into your account menu, and a tidier library
updates.summary: E-mail alerts now live in your account menu (click your name, top right) rather than the top navigation. In My Library the paper-search filters are hidden (the library has its own search), and clicking an active list or tag now deactivates it — back to all saved — without removing it.

updates.title: Data Analytics: compare journals against their peer groups
updates.summary: When you pick specific journals, every corpus chart now overlays the aggregate behaviour of the journal types those journals belong to (UTD24, FT50, ABS 4/4*, ABS 3) — as comparison lines on the time-series charts and comparison bars/lines on the others — with toggle buttons under each chart to switch each peer group on or off. The publication-volume chart also lets you click a legend entry to hide or show a line, and both time-series charts trim the year axis to when the shown journals actually have papers. The dashboard now opens on the entire database by default.

updates.title: Data Analytics: compare authors, cleaner counts, native-first default
updates.summary: The Author spotlight now lets you add several authors and compare them side by side: publications per year, where they publish, and an at-a-glance table with collaboration statistics — average co-authors per paper, how cited their co-authors are, average citations per paper, and mean disruption — each shown both under your current filters and across all their papers in the database. A pre-ticked 'exclude non-research items' switch keeps journal front matter, book reviews, corrigenda, announcements and indices out of every figure (the main database still shows everything). The dashboard also opens on the curated native sources by default, with catalog journals added on selection.

updates.title: Editor & area filters in Data Analytics
updates.summary: The Data Analytics dashboard now offers the same editorial filters as the main browser: choose Management Science to filter by accepting editor or editorial area (or Information Systems Research / Marketing Science to filter by senior/associate editor), and every tile and chart — publication volume, co-authorship, citations, most-cited — updates to that slice, so you can chart one area's papers over time.

updates.title: INFORMS Transactions on Education added
updates.summary: The Lit now crawls INFORMS Transactions on Education (INFORMS), the open-access education-science journal for OR/MS/analytics, as a core source. Its full archive and Articles in Advance are searchable with every filter, and new papers are picked up automatically.

updates.title: Strategy Science added
updates.summary: The Lit now crawls Strategy Science (INFORMS), adding it as a core source alongside Management Science, Operations Research, Marketing Science, M&SOM, Information Systems Research and POM. Its full back-catalogue and Articles in Advance are searchable with every filter, it is grouped under the ABS 3 journal type, and new papers are picked up automatically.

updates.title: Econometrica forthcoming papers, tracked
updates.summary: The Lit now follows Econometrica's Forthcoming Papers page and checks the journal for newly-published work every 20 minutes (on top of the daily rebuild), so accepted-but-not-yet-in-an-issue Econometrica papers show up — and join “Recently added” — soon after they are posted, instead of the next day.

updates.title: New papers appear within minutes — with pre-print & citations
updates.summary: The six Articles-in-Advance journals (Management Science, Operations Research, Marketing Science, M&SOM, Information Systems Research and POM) are now checked for freshly-published papers every 15 minutes, on top of the full daily rebuild — so a new Article in Advance shows up (and joins “Recently added”) usually within minutes of Crossref indexing it, instead of the next day. Each newly-added paper is also enriched on arrival, so its open-access pre-print link and citation count are there from the start rather than filling in hours later.

updates.title: Filling in missing author names
updates.summary: Some older papers (and certain publishers) arrive from Crossref with no author names; the daily citations sweep now backfills those from OpenAlex, so more cards — and more entries in the “Cited references in this catalog” panel — show who wrote each paper. It fills in gradually over the coming days.

updates.title: Cited references now show their authors
updates.summary: In the “Cited references in this catalog” panel, each cited paper now lists its authors under the title, so you can see who wrote it without opening it.

updates.title: Cited-references toggle now shows a count
updates.summary: The “Cited references in this catalog” toggle on each paper now displays how many in-catalog references that paper cites — e.g. “Cited 12 references in this catalog” — so you can see the number before expanding it.

updates.title: Team science: which work disrupts, and which develops
updates.summary: The Data Analytics page now reproduces the key measures of Wu, Wang & Evans (Nature 2019): a disruption index D for each paper and author, and how disruption, impact, and search depth change with team size — all filterable by journal, type, year, and a new Disruptiveness-index range slider (−1 to 1), and sharpening as the citation graph grows.

updates.title: Papers now show their citation counts
updates.summary: Every paper card carries a “Cited by N” badge — the highest of the Crossref, OpenAlex and Semantic Scholar tallies — that links through to the paper on Google Scholar.

updates.title: Walk the citation graph inside the catalog
updates.summary: A “Cited references in this catalog” toggle on each paper lists the other papers it cites that are themselves in The Lit, each linking straight to that paper.

updates.title: Working Papers — the listed authors’ unpublished work
updates.summary: A new Working Papers journal type surfaces the unpublished pre-prints (SSRN, NBER, arXiv, OSF) of every author already in the catalog.

updates.title: A Data Analytics dashboard
updates.summary: An interactive summary of the whole corpus — publication volume over time, co-authorship, pre-print and citation trends, an author spotlight and an editorial breakdown you can click to filter.

updates.title: Save your default filters
updates.summary: Signed-in users can save a preferred set of journals or journal types that the page applies automatically each time they sign in.

updates.title: Open-access pre-print links on every paper
updates.summary: Wherever a free author pre-print exists on arXiv, SSRN, NBER, OSF, bioRxiv or medRxiv, the card links straight to the PDF, latest version.

updates.title: Journal types: UTD24, FT50 and ABS
updates.summary: Filter by UTD24, FT50, ABS 4/4* and ABS 3, with a badge on each card showing the most selective list its journal belongs to.

updates.title: E-mail alerts
updates.summary: Subscribe to an e-mail when new papers matching your filters are added — or about any new paper, or about new features on the site.

updates.title: A private library when you sign in
updates.summary: Star papers, add notes, build lists and tag your reading — all private to your account.
